title: host-asterinas-audit: feature gap analysis against beads + asterinas tree
body:
# Goal

Walk the asterinas-workspace issue tree end-to-end and
produce a feature-audit doc that pins:

1. Every convention asterinas uses today (status values,
   workflow tags, hierarchy encoding, dep edges, ad-hoc
   header fields).
2. The cleanest jjforge representation today (label?
   parent-child? built-in field?).
3. The beads-feature candidate that would express it
   better, if any.
4. A verdict per convention: ship the feature / use a
   label / skip / not applicable.

End state: a markdown doc at `docs/host-asterinas-audit.md`
that lists every convention, and a set of follow-up
implementation tickets filed for the features the audit
identifies as worth shipping. Each follow-up has a
parent-child edge back to the
`epic:host-asterinas` epic (`ec5a0a8`).

# Why now

User direction: bend asterinas to fit beads-style
features, not the other way around. The asterinas tree
(~968 issues, ~122 directories, multi-section issue
bodies) is the live testbed. Whatever jjforge can't
express queriably gets a feature ticket.

We just shipped the snapshot cache, so reading at this
scale is no longer a perf concern. Migration is unblocked
on perf, but blocked on schema: today jjforge can express
type + slug + status + labels + dep-edges (4 kinds) +
block reason + memories + assignee + body + comments.
We'll find out what's missing.

# Scope

## What to read

- `~/p/asterinas-workspace/issues/README.md` — the
  workspace map.
- `~/p/asterinas-workspace/issues/phases.md` — the
  multi-phase roadmap structure.
- A representative sample across every phase, every
  cross-cutting topic, and every epic README. ~30-50
  issues, picked for variety not volume.
- Every distinct `**Status:**` value, every
  `**Workflow:**` tag, every header field that appears
  more than once.

## What to compare against

- `reference/beads/` — every feature it ships that we
  don't. The big ones: `bd remember` (we shipped),
  `bd dep --kind <kind>` (we shipped), atomic claim (we
  shipped), `bd defer` + `defer_until`, `bd await`
  + `await_type/await_id` (we shipped a strict subset
  as `Status::Blocked`), `bd metadata` (arbitrary JSON
  blob per issue), hierarchical IDs (`bd-abc.1.2`),
  `bd federation` (multi-repo), `bd priority`
  (numeric 0–4), `bd convoy` / `bd molecule` (multi-agent
  orchestration shapes), `bd hooked` (external gate),
  `bd issue-type` (12 types vs our 6).
- `reference/beads/AGENTS.md` and
  `reference/beads/AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md` — the agent-
  loop primitives.

## Output

`docs/host-asterinas-audit.md`, structured as a table:

| asterinas convention | example | current jjforge mapping | beads alternative | verdict | follow-up ticket |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

Plus a "Recommendation" section at the end:

- Features to ship: list with one-line justification.
- Features to skip: list with one-line why-not.
- Asterinas conventions that lose information either
  way: list with the loss documented.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `docs/host-asterinas-audit.md` exists.
- [ ] Every distinct `**Status:**` value in the asterinas
  tree is in the table with a mapping.
- [ ] Every distinct `**Workflow:**` tag is in the table.
- [ ] The phase → epic → ticket hierarchy gets a verdict
  (parent-child edges via the new dep-types? Labels?
  Both?).
- [ ] Follow-up implementation tickets filed for every
  "ship the feature" verdict. Each has:
  - `--type feature`
  - `--slug host-asterinas-<feature>` (so they sort
    together in `jjf ls --slug host-asterinas`)
  - `-l epic:host-asterinas`
  - A `parent-child` dep edge to `ec5a0a8` via
    `jjf dep add <new-id> ec5a0a8 --kind parent-child`.
- [ ] The audit doc lands in a single commit with the
  follow-up tickets filed on the bookmark separately.

# Out of scope

- Implementing any feature. This is research → follow-up
  tickets. Other subagents implement the followups.
- Touching asterinas-workspace itself. We're reading
  it, not editing it.
- The migration itself. That's `host-asterinas-migrate`,
  filed separately, blocked on the feature work landing.

# Pointers

- Source tree: `~/p/asterinas-workspace/issues/`.
- Audit doc location: `docs/host-asterinas-audit.md`.
- Parent epic: `ec5a0a8`. Use
  `jjf dep add <new-id> ec5a0a8 --kind parent-child`
  to link each follow-up.
- Beads reference (read-only): `reference/beads/`.
- jjforge's current spec: `docs/storage-format.md` at v2.5.
EOF
